subject: Re: strange and rare failures in fs_motion_and_ctf
body:
Hi Daniel,

This seems like quite a minor (although clearly annoying!) bug which
doesn't appear to be blocking you or affecting too many people so isn't a
high priority to fix right now, apologies!

Cheers,

Alister

On Thu, 13 Mar 2025 at 10:25, Daniel Cai <dxhcai@gmail.com> wrote:

> Hi Alister,
>
> I did another run on the same frameseries using the same parameters and
> checked the crash files. Strangely warp processing is not failing 100%
> consistently - 5/4500 failed first run, 4/4500 failed second run. 3/5 that
> failed in the first run failed in the second run.
>
> I also didn't notice anything unusual in the failed frames in terms of
> contrast, darkness, ice, etc. I've had much worse frames pass this with no
> issue.
>
> I can share the frameseries if you want to test them?
>
> Thanks,
> Daniel
>
>
>
> On Wed, Mar 12, 2025 at 4:37 PM Alister Burt <alisterburt@gmail.com>
> wrote:
>
>> Hi Daniel,
>>
>> Hm - you mention rare, is there anything obviously different about the
>> images it fails on?
>>
>> Cheers,
>>
>> Alister
>>
>> On Wed, 12 Mar 2025 at 09:40, Daniel Cai <dxhcai@gmail.com> wrote:
>>
>>> Hi everyone,
>>>
>>> I occasionally encounter this strange error which prevents certain frame
>>> series from being processed by fs_motion_and_ctf. Looking into old
>>> messages, it seems a similar error is documented in Windows M and Warp as
>>> well as the linux build.
>>>
>>> Not entirely sure what is causing the error, there doesn't seem to be a
>>> theme for the files. Frames themselves seem normal...
>>>
>>> If anyone else has encountered this error, could you let me know how you
>>> solved it?
>>>
>>> Best,
>>> Daniel
>>>
>>> Here is the error message:
>>>
>>> System.OverflowException: Value was either too large or too small for a
>>> Decimal.
>>> at System.Number.ThrowOverflowException(String message)
>>> at System.Decimal.DecCalc.VarDecFromR8(Double input, DecCalc& result)
>>> at Warp.Movie.<>c.<ProcessCTF>b__314_13(Double[] input, CTF ctf,
>>> Single[] defocusValues, Single[] phaseValues) in
>>> /opt/warp/WarpLib/Movie.cs:line 1710
>>> at Warp.Movie.<>c__DisplayClass314_4.<ProcessCTF>b__14(Double[] input)
>>> in /opt/warp/WarpLib/Movie.cs:line 1737
>>> at Accord.Math.Optimization.LBFGS.line_search_morethuente(Int32 n,
>>> Double[] x, Double& f, Double[] g, Double[] s, Double& stp, Double[] xp,
>>> Double[] gp, Double[] wa, callback_data_t& cd, lbfgs_parameter_t& param)
>>> at Accord.Math.Optimization.LBFGS.lbfgs(Int32 n, Double[] x, Double&
>>> ptr_fx, lbfgs_evaluate_t proc_evaluate, lbfgs_progress_t proc_progress,
>>> Object instance, lbfgs_parameter_t _param)
>>> at Accord.Math.Optimization.BroydenFletcherGoldfarbShanno.Optimize()
>>> at Accord.Math.Optimization.BaseOptimizationMethod.Maximize()
>>> at Accord.Math.Optimization.BaseGradientOptimizationMethod.Maximize()
>>> at Warp.Movie.ProcessCTF(Image originalStack, ProcessingOptionsMovieCTF
>>> options) in /opt/warp/WarpLib/Movie.cs:line 1902
>>> at WarpWorker.WarpWorker.EvaluateCommand(NamedSerializableObject
>>> Command) in /opt/warp/WarpWorker/WarpWorker.cs:line 265
>>>
>>> --
>>> You received this message because you are subscribed to the Google
>>> Groups "Warp" group.
>>> To unsubscribe from this group and stop receiving emails from it, send
>>> an email to warp-em+unsubscribe@googlegroups.com.
>>> To view this discussion visit
>>> https://groups.google.com/d/msgid/warp-em/7bbb71a2-08fc-43b6-91f0-e8e40dd2be07n%40googlegroups.com
>>> <https://groups.google.com/d/msgid/warp-em/7bbb71a2-08fc-43b6-91f0-e8e40dd2be07n%40googlegroups.com?utm_medium=email&utm_source=footer>
>>> .
>>>
>>
